reasoning_content: We need to determine if 439 is prime. Check divisibility: not even, sum of digits 4+3+9=16, not divisible by 3. Ends with 9, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*62=434, remainder 5, so not. 11: 11*39=429, remainder 10; 11*40=440, too high. 13: 13*33=429, remainder 10; 13*34=442, no. 17: 17*25=425, remainder 14; 17*26=442, no. 19: 19*23=437, remainder 2; 19*24=456, too high. 23: 23*19=437, remainder 2. 29: 29*15=435, remainder 4; 29*16=464, too high. 31: 31*14=434, remainder 5. 37: 37*11=407, remainder 32; 37*12=444, too high. 41: 41*10=410, remainder 29; 41*11=451, too high. 43: 43*10=430, remainder 9; 43*11=473. Up to sqrt(439) ~ 20.95, so we need primes up to 19. Already checked 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. None divide evenly. So 439 is prime. Answer: yes.
